title: Rejections carry literal next calls
description: possible_repairs and did_you_mean as complete, paste-ready op JSON, never prose advice or bare names. Copying beats composing for weak emitters; this is the constrained decoder for stacks without grammars. Partially done (repairs work started); finish to the executable-verbatim bar. cross-model.md engine #1.
notes:
Chunk 1 done: Repair type, view addressing-miss repairs (pkg/sym/receiver/method/field cases), executable-verbatim filter, daemon wire plumbing, spec updated. Next chunks: patch-op addressing misses, stale-generation view repair, unknown-op substitution, help-call fallback for shape errors.
Chunks 2-3 done: stale-generation view repair, unknown-op corrected-patch repair (no repair on catalog fallback), unknown-handle view repair, envelope sym-miss view repairs. Remaining: in-op symbol substitution (rename/set_body targeting missing syms), position/kind keyword substitution, help-call fallback for op-shape errors, init.go teaching text mentions possible_repairs.
Chunks 4-6 done: where/with keyword substitution, in-op sym/pkg addressing substitution with resolution filter, init.go teaches paste-the-repair. Remaining for close: single-op sugar paths (rename/set_body/add_param direct calls carry no repairs yet), help-call fallback for op-shape errors, semantic-diagnostic repairs (undefined ident near-miss).
All planned classes done: sugar paths (rename/set_body/add_param), query tier, help fallback for missing required args, undefined-ident search repairs at all seven typecheck reject sites. Deliberately no repair: semantic guesses beyond search, upsert_decl pkg miss (creates packages on demand), catalog-dump fallbacks. Close after a bench round proves repair uptake.

title: Daemon request log: -log flag + JSONL in handle()
description: General daemon option, off by default: op, args digest, outcome, rejection reason, repairs offered, generation, latency per request as JSONL. Must reach the auto-spawned daemon (dial() and MCP command paths); env var simplest. Unlocks op-mix, resubmission, repair-uptake, stale-gen, time-to-first-mutation counters. Proposal section 3.

title: Serving profiles: bench/profiles.json + loader
description: Profile struct: endpoint, model id, sampler (repeat penalty explicitly 1.0), thinking/effort, quant label, context size, notes. Load in setup(), embed whole profile in run.json, res[profile] per episode. Server identity (build probe, model hash) folds in here. Sub-item: sampler wiring into opencode provider options. Proposal section 2.

title: failure_kind episode taxonomy
description: Replace agent_error bool with failure_kind: capped|endpoint_error|harness_crash|tool_parse|scored_fail. classifyFailure(agentErr, timedOut, agentOut, pass) in bench_test.go episode(); derive markers from real transcripts under bench/results/. Proposal section 1.

title: Rework bench to match framework proposal
description: Bring bench/ in line with docs/specs/bench-framework-proposal.md: taxonomy, per-model profiles, daemon evidence, oracle arm, both harnesses first-class (opencode + Pi via MCP extension), MLflow derived comparison layer, log placement and token accounting per the resolved proposal. Break into small chunks; delta map from Explore agent drives the order.

title: Decl ops: move_decl, set_signature, remove_param, implement_interface, add_bench
description: Deferred from the language plan. set_signature rejects with the call-site list unless sites are repaired in the same patch (spec line 102). implement_interface and add_bench round out the decl tier.
notes: Priority raised: 24 mined move tasks and 1 signature task in bench/tasks.json are blocked on move_decl and set_signature. These ops are the path to complex bench cases beyond rename/add-param.

title: Run the bench roster
description: 10 cells per cross-model.md roster table. Fill order when time-boxed: GLM (wired), gpt-oss-20b, Qwen3.6-27B, gpt-oss-120b, Coder-Next, Gemma 4, DeepSeek, Devstral pair, 8B floor last. 8B cell scored separately (raw mode near-certain zero; smoke one episode before committing a k). Gemma 4 is the generalist arm, reversing the README exclusion. Qwen 8B/27B/80B forms the within-family scale curve.

title: Episode counters in JSONL
description: Tool-call parse failures, JSON decode failures (and whether lenient repair fixed them), op mix with per-op accept/reject, identical-resubmission count, tokens in/out per turn, time to first accepted mutation. Only way to see which optimizations matter per family. cross-model.md bench #2.

title: Per-model serving profiles
description: One named profile per model: parser, template revision, sampler, quant, thinking/effort, grammar on/off. Pinned in manifest, recorded in episode metadata. Repetition penalty off as lineup default. cross-model.md bench #1.

title: Determinism as a tested invariant
description: Identical query against unchanged snapshot returns identical bytes: sorted keys, stable ordering, no timestamps or volatile counters. Prefix caching on every stack hangs on this. Test: run every query twice against the fixture, assert byte equality. view_test.go has one determinism check; extend to all queries. cross-model.md engine #3.

title: Bounded list responses
description: refs/callers/search capped with total count and explicit truncation marker, pageable. Keeps a popular symbol from dumping 400 sites into a weak model's context. Partial truncation exists in snapshot.go; verify caps, count, marker, paging. cross-model.md engine #4.

title: Accept responses carry fresh {generation, view}
description: Mutations return {generation, view} for the touched declaration so edit-then-edit-again needs no view call between patches. Cheapest structural token win. cross-model.md engine #2.

title: Near-miss matching misses renamed symbols
description: Smoke run: after Shared.extractLabels -> extractSwarmLabels, a refs/rename against the old name got no did_you_mean and no repairs; substring matching cannot see extractLabels inside extractSwarmLabels. Add subsequence or edit-distance matching to suggestSymbols so post-rename stale names still produce candidates.

title: Repair filename-as-sym addressing
description: Smoke run 20260715-201514: GLM addressed view with sym=pswarm.go and pswarm.go:232 (filename and file:line), rejected with zero repairs. Emit a redirect repair: strip extension/position, send the search query call for the basename fragment, plus a one-line note that syms are pkg.Sym addresses not files.
notes: Shipped: fileAddressRepair on view and query addressing misses; search call for the file base name.

title: make README code compile. Never gets out of sync.
Prompt parity for raw mode + token counts in run.json
description: Raw mode gets an equivalently sized workflow paragraph in its own idioms (grep, edit, build). Record both prompts' token counts in run.json (heuristic count fine). Proposal section 6.

title: bench report subcommand
description: Read run dirs, aggregate episodes.jsonl by task/mode/profile: pass@k with binomial CI (Wilson), median time-to-green, capped counts, failure_kind breakdown, counter summaries. Markdown + completion-curve CSV. Proposal section 5.

title: Per-kind goal predicate dispatch
description: Add Kind to runner Manifest (unify with cmd/bench/prep.go copy), map[string]predicateFn, rename logic becomes renamePredicate. add-param/signature/move/wrap-error predicates arrive with their prep extractors. Proposal section 4.

title: Oracle arm as recorded pseudo-mode
description: Oracle harness does not exist in code yet: build it rename-first (replay ground-truth via ago rename per spec), add oracle to the modes loop, score identically. Proves protocol-solvability, gives time-to-green floor, produces SFT corpus. Depends on kind dispatch. Proposal section 4.

title: Token accounting per episode
description: tokens in/out (+reasoning, cache) and cost per episode. Source: opencode sqlite by session id vs --format json stream; decide on next smoke run; session id capture is prerequisite. Proposal section 3.

title: Bench collects daemon request log per episode
description: Point daemon log at per-workspace path in writeOpencodeConfig/runAgent; record() copies requests.jsonl into epDir. Semantic-mode-only evidence. Depends on daemon -log.

title: Canary probe with restart-on-mismatch (bench)
description: Fixed completion against endpoint before each episode, compared to known-good; on mismatch run AGO_BENCH_RESTART_CMD, re-probe once, record res[canary]. Per-episode cadence first; GLM mid-episode heartbeat deferred. Supersedes agent-go-hao scope for the runner. Proposal section 1.

title: Matrix loop over AGO_BENCH_PROFILES
description: Outer loop over named profiles in BenchmarkRename, profile name in b.Run path. Concurrent endpoints deferred. Proposal section 2.

title: Front coverage: daemon dispatch and CLI verbs
description: internal/snapshot is at 80.9% coverage; the fronts are thin: internal/daemon 23.3%, cmd/ago 37.4%, cmd/bench 0%. All ops route through the daemon switch, so one fixture-driven test walking every verb through the real CLI/daemon path lifts both and guards the routing constraint from the plan. internal/protocol is 20 lines of types, no tests needed.

title: Project ops: add_dependency, remove_dependency, move_file, delete_file, mod_tidy
description: The project tier from the spec op table, deferred from the language plan.

title: Agent language spec
description: From todo.md: agent language spec, whatever that looks like.

title: Docs: language for local models (humans and agents)
description: From todo.md: docs aimed at humans and at agents.

title: AGENTS.md common skeleton with per-family variants
description: ago init writes protocol instructions: short, imperative, example-led, one worked query-view-patch-reject-repair loop, explicit no-shell line. One common skeleton with named per-family register variants, not five hand-maintained files. cross-model.md.

title: Devstral 123B decode-speed check
description: Measure real decode t/s at Q4 on the 128GB Mac before any 123B bench run; the number decides k=3 vs k=1 for the adversarial cell. Gates the Devstral 123B roster row. devstral.md / README open item.

title: Prompt prefix hygiene
description: No worktree paths, task ids, or dates in system prompt or tool catalog; stable tool ordering in tools/list; byte-stable help per catalog version. Lets k=3 episodes share a warm cache. cross-model.md bench #4.

title: Harness resend audit
description: Per-stack checklists: tool results resent byte-identical, results on the tool role (never a second user message), reasoning-block handling per model rule. cross-model.md bench #5.

title: Canary probe with restart-on-mismatch
description: Fixed probe completion before each episode checked against known-good output; restart server on failure. Motivated by GLM wedge-while-healthy; health-check-lies is a risk on every stack. cross-model.md bench #3.

title: Help examples validated against fixture
description: Every catalog entry carries one minimal patch accepted against the fixture. Task 12 asserts examples parse; extend to would-validate. cross-model.md engine #8.

title: Unknown-tool/op aliases and no-shell redirect
description: Alias obvious name variants (ago_view, get_view); answer hallucinated shell-ish tools (bash, str_replace, execute_command) with one-line redirect: no shell here, mutations go through patch ops. cross-model.md engine #7.

title: Resubmission circuit breaker
description: Hash rejected patches; exact resend of a just-rejected patch gets an escalated rejection: shorter reason, harder imperative naming the recovery call. cross-model.md engine #6.
notes: Shipped daemon-wide, not patch-only: resendBreaker escalates any exact resend of a rejected request with resent count and hard imperative. Evidence: episode 20260715-210756 resent one rejected view 23 times.

title: Lenient patch decode, repair noted
description: Accept literal newlines and tabs inside JSON strings, trailing commas, single-quoted keys; report the normalization in the response. Absorbs the most common damage class from small models in code-bearing string args. cross-model.md engine #5.

title: Harness axis: record + Pi driver
description: res[harness] label first. Then driver interface (opencode|pi), Pi via MCP-server extension route, purity flags, thinking dial, token extraction from Pi session JSONL. Sequenced late: rename round, one model first. Proposal Harness section.

title: bench export --mlflow
description: One MLflow run per episode nested under bench run via OTel Go SDK to OTLP /v1/traces. Params profile+mode+harness+task; metrics pass/predicate/wall/tokens/counters; idempotent, one-way from git. Gated on counters existing. Proposal MLflow section.

title: Contamination caveat in bench.md
description: Mode-vs-mode comparison holds contamination constant; it inflates absolute rates; recency-mining is the escape hatch. Doc-only. Proposal section 7.

title: SSA query tier
description: Queries are static-only in v1. Includes callers/callees candidate expansion through method sets (dc65d0f). Deferred from the language plan.

title: Handle migration on stale generations
description: Today a moved generation forces re-view; migrate handles across generations where the decl body is unchanged. Deferred from the language plan.

title: Structured expression nodes
description: Grammar frozen in language.md (ident/select/index/call/lit/unary/binary/paren/func); v1 accepts text atoms only. Target for constrained decoding: one JSON grammar covers a whole patch. Consumers are the per-stack grammar integrations; nothing universal waits on it (cross-model.md non-builds).

title: Make it pretty
description: From todo.md; scope undefined.

title: MiMo-V2-Flash optimization doc
description: Borderline roster candidate (309B/15B active, MTP, claims top open-source SWE-bench Verified). Same size class as DeepSeek V4 Flash; viable only via aggressive selective quant. Write the doc if community quants prove out at ~80GB. optimizations/README.md.

title: Oracle harness as training-data generator
description: Oracle solutions rendered as episode transcripts give SFT data; rejected-then-repaired pairs give preference data; perturbed oracle patches give rejection-recovery data. Rendering differs per chat format; generator is one piece of work. cross-model.md long game.

title: No markdown fences in protocol output
description: Plain text everywhere in views, help, rejections; models mirror formatting. Grep shows none today; add a guard test so it stays true. cross-model.md engine #9.
